// description: Golden reference for the sddp_hydro_3phase integration case.
Captured from a converged iter=50 low_memory=off run.
`tools/validate_sddp_output.py --golden-json` asserts the current
run matches these values to `tolerance`.
Reduced pin set (2026-07-08): this fixture's optimum is PRIMAL
DEGENERATE — hydro (gcost 5) substitutes thermal (gcost 50) at a
constant 45/MWh saving in every phase, so the marginal water value
is flat and ANY allocation of the reservoir budget across phases
and blocks is an alternate optimum with the same total objective.
The per-phase obj_value tails, the intermediate reservoir efin,
and the per-block dispatch all sit on that flat face and drift
with solver pivot order, low_memory mode, and cut-path details.
The base golden therefore pins only solver-invariant quantities:
the TOTAL objective / LB / UB / gap, per-phase solve statuses,
the drained horizon efin (leftover water always has positive
substitution value, so every optimum drains), and the phase-1 bus
balance dual (thermal is strictly interior, so the marginal cost
is its gcost).  The per-solver variants
(golden_iter50_<solver>.json) adopted this reduced set first; see
golden_iter50_hotstart_clp.json for the original diagnosis.
// objective_value: Total run objective — checked against solver_status.upper_bound when that file is present, else against solution.csv phase-1 obj_value.
// solution_csv_rows: Phase-1 obj_value is the cumulative total (strict pin).  Phases 2-3
pin status only — their obj_value tails are the degenerate
